title: Fix signed enum false wrap-around error when crossing zero
body:
## Summary

This patch fixes the enum auto-increment wrap-around detection to correctly
handle signed base types. The existing `isEqZero()` check in `V3Width.cpp`
produces a false `"Enum value illegally wrapped around (IEEE 1800-2023 6.19)"`
error for signed enums that legitimately increment through zero from negative
starting values.

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t;
  typedef enum int {
    A = -2,
    B,  // -1
    C,  // 0  <-- false error here
    D   // 1
  } signed_e;

  initial begin
    if (A !== -2 || B !== -1 || C !== 0 || D !== 1) $stop;
    $write("*-* All Finished *-*\n");
    $finish;
  end
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error: t.v:5:5: Enum value illegally wrapped around (IEEE 1800-2023 6.19)
```

## Changes

- Replaced inline `num.isEqZero()` wrap-around check with a `wrapAround` flag
  computed after each `opAdd()` in the enum item loop (`V3Width.cpp`)
- Signed types: detect positive-to-negative overflow (`!prevNeg && num.isNegative()`)
- Unsigned types: preserve original zero-check behavior (`num.isEqZero()`)
- Explicit enum value assignments reset the flag

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_enum_signed_wrap.v` + `.py` -- six signed enum
  scenarios (cross-zero, start-at-zero, all-negative, 8-bit signed, single
  value, from-negative-one) with runtime value assertions

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
